facts.label: BORN A SOCCEROO
facts.story: Sydney journalist Tony Horstead coined 'Socceroos' in 1967 while covering a squad's goodwill tour of South Vietnam, though his own newspaper poll had readers voting for 'Emus' instead. The nickname stuck anyway: the Australian Soccer Federation's 1972 World Cup fundraising drive stamped a kangaroo in football boots with 'World Cup 1974 Socceroo,' and by kickoff in West Germany it was universal. The green and gold itself dates back further, to 1924, when officials swapped Australia's original sky-blue-and-maroon strip for colors drawn from the Golden Wattle and the eucalypt bush.

facts.label: THE 32-YEAR WAIT
facts.story: Australia's 1974 squad was entirely amateur — office workers and tradesmen moonlighting as footballers — and went home from West Germany without a single goal in three matches. The Socceroos didn't return to the World Cup for 32 years. In Kaiserslautern in 2006, trailing Japan 1-0 with 20 minutes left, substitute Tim Cahill scored twice in five second-half minutes, and John Aloisi added a third in stoppage time. The 3-1 comeback delivered Australia's first-ever World Cup win, on their second attempt.

1974.headline: The Scoreless Debut
1974.homeColors: gold shirt, green shorts, white socks

2006.headline: Cahill's Late Arrival

annotations.label: CAHILL'S FIVE MINUTES
annotations.story: Down 1-0 to Japan with 20 minutes left in Kaiserslautern, substitute Tim Cahill leveled in the 84th minute, then struck again in the 89th. John Aloisi added a third in stoppage time. The 3-1 win was Australia's first-ever World Cup victory, 32 years after their scoreless 1974 debut.

annotations.label: KEWELL'S LATE LEVELER
annotations.story: Trailing Croatia 2-1 with ten minutes left, Harry Kewell drilled a loose ball past Stipe Pletikosa in the 79th minute for a 2-2 draw. The goal was nearly overshadowed by referee Graham Poll's blunder minutes earlier — booking Josip Šimunić three times before sending him off — but it sent Australia through to the round of 16.

2006.homeColors: gold shirt with green trim, green shorts, gold socks (Nike)

2010.headline: Kewell's Costly Red Card

annotations.label: TEN MEN HOLD ON
annotations.story: Brett Holman pounced on a Ghana goalkeeping error to put Australia ahead in the 11th minute. Harry Kewell then blocked a goal-bound shot with his upper arm, was sent off, and Asamoah Gyan converted the penalty to level it. Australia held on with ten men for a 1-1 draw that kept their knockout hopes alive.

2010.homeColors: gold shirt, navy shorts, gold socks (Nike)

2014.headline: Cahill's Wonder Strike

annotations.label: THE UNDERSIDE OF THE BAR
annotations.story: One minute after Arjen Robben's opener, Tim Cahill met a long ball from Ryan McGowan and volleyed it in off the underside of the crossbar — instantly ranked among the tournament's best goals. Australia briefly led 2-1 after Mile Jedinak's penalty, but Robin van Persie and Memphis Depay answered to complete a 3-2 Dutch win.

2014.homeColors: gold shirt with green sleeves, navy shorts, gold socks (Nike)

2018.headline: So Close, Group Stage Again

annotations.label: VAR'S FIRST WORLD CUP CALL
annotations.story: Antoine Griezmann converted the first-ever VAR-assisted penalty in World Cup history in the 58th minute. Mile Jedinak answered from the spot four minutes later to level it at 1-1, before a shot from Paul Pogba deflected in off Aziz Behich in the 81st minute, handing France a 2-1 win.

2018.homeColors: gold shirt, green shorts, gold socks (Nike)

2022.headline: Argentina Ends the Run

annotations.label: GOODWIN STRIKES EARLY
annotations.story: Craig Goodwin lashed a left-footed strike into the roof of the net in the ninth minute to briefly threaten the eventual champions. France answered with four unanswered goals, but the early setback didn't define the group — Australia still finished above Denmark and reached the knockout stage for a second time.

annotations.label: LECKIE SENDS THEM THROUGH
annotations.story: Mathew Leckie collected a long ball, drove into the box, and beat Kasper Schmeichel at the far post in the 60th minute to sink Denmark 1-0. The win put Australia through to the round of 16 for just the second time, level with 2006 as their best-ever World Cup finish.

annotations.label: GOODWIN'S HISTORIC DEFLECTION
annotations.story: Trailing 2-0, Craig Goodwin's low shot deflected off Enzo Fernández and past goalkeeper Emiliano Martínez in the 77th minute — Australia's first-ever goal in a World Cup knockout match. It wasn't enough; Argentina held on 2-1 and went on to win the tournament.

2022.homeColors: gold shirt with a stitched Golden Wattle pattern, green shorts, gold socks (Nike)
2022.awayColors: obsidian dark shirt with green accents (Nike)
